title: Flow If/Else (LLM)
description: Two-branch conditional router whose decision is made by a wired LLM.
The condition is a natural-language yes/no question (e.g. 'Is there a
dog in this image?'). The chunk and the question are sent to the LLM
via the control-plane invoke channel; the LLM is instructed to reply
with one word YES or NO. YES → THEN, anything else → ELSE. Wire a
text LLM on the 'llm' channel for text lanes, and a vision-capable
LLM on the 'vision' channel for image lanes. Audio and video need
preprocessing (STT, frame grabber) upstream — LLM APIs do not accept

llm.description: Text LLM that evaluates the natural-language condition for text-family lanes (text, documents, questions, answers, ...). Connect any llm_* node. Required when routing text lanes; ignored for image lanes.

vision.description: Vision-capable LLM that evaluates the natural-language condition for image lanes. Connect any llm_vision_* node. Required when routing image lanes; ignored for text lanes.

default.title: Default
default.flow_llm.condition: Does the content match the criterion?

flow_llm.condition.title: Question
flow_llm.condition.description: Natural-language yes/no question evaluated by the connected LLM. The chunk being routed is provided as context. Write ONLY the question — the node automatically prepends the routing-classifier system prompt and appends the 'Reply with EXACTLY one word: YES or NO' instruction. Do not add 'answer YES/NO' yourself. Examples: 'Is this image a screenshot?', 'Is this text in Spanish?', 'Does the image contain a person?'. Be specific.
flow_llm.condition.default: Does the content match the criterion?

flow_llm.timeout_s.title: Timeout (seconds)
flow_llm.timeout_s.description: Maximum wall-clock time per evaluation. LLM inference is much slower than sandbox eval; default 30s. Timeout routes to ELSE.

flow_llm.branches.title: Branch targets
flow_llm.branches.description: Maps each branch name to the list of downstream node IDs on that branch. Populated automatically by the canvas when you wire edges from the THEN / ELSE output ports.
